prerequisites: A running ClickHouse server (self-hosted or ClickHouse Cloud) reachable from the connector at the configured host and port.
A ClickHouse user account with a password that has permission to write to the target database.
The target database created in ClickHouse, supplied via the required `database` field.
Network connectivity to the chosen protocol port: the native TCP protocol defaults to 9000 (9440 with TLS) and the HTTP interface defaults to 8123 (8443 with TLS).
If the server is not directly reachable, an SSH bastion host for the optional `tunnel_method` (SSH key or SSH password).

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: ClickHouse authenticates clients with a username and password over its native or HTTP interface. Provide the account in the `username` field and its secret in the `password` field. Use an existing user (for example the `default` account) or create one with `CREATE USER <name> IDENTIFIED BY '<password>'` and grant it access to the target database.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel (optional)
authMethods.summary: When ClickHouse is not directly reachable, the optional `tunnel_method` field routes the connection through an SSH bastion. Authenticate to the bastion with either an SSH private key (`tunnel_method.ssh_key`) or an SSH user password (`tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password`). This secures the transport to the host but does not replace the ClickHouse username and password.

setupSteps.title: Prepare a ClickHouse user and database
setupSteps.body: In ClickHouse, create or identify the target database and a user that can write to it. To create a dedicated user, run `CREATE USER <name> IDENTIFIED BY '<password>'` and grant it the needed privileges with `GRANT`. SQL-driven user management requires `access_management` enabled for the administering account.

setupSteps.title: Determine host, protocol, and port
setupSteps.body: Set `host` to the ClickHouse server hostname or IP. Choose `protocol` (native TCP or HTTP) and set `port` to match: native TCP defaults to 9000 (9440 with TLS), and the HTTP interface defaults to 8123 (8443 with TLS).

setupSteps.title: Enter credentials and connection config
setupSteps.body: Provide the required fields: `host`, `port`, `protocol`, `database`, `username`, and `password`. Optionally set `enable_json` and `record_window_size` to tune behavior.

setupSteps.title: Configure an SSH tunnel if required
setupSteps.body: If ClickHouse is behind a bastion, set `tunnel_method` and supply the bastion credentials: an SSH private key (`tunnel_method.ssh_key`) or an SSH user password (`tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password`).

sources.title: Access control and account management - ClickHouse Docs
